title: Define rules engine types and trigger evaluation
purpose: Define the rules engine data model and trigger evaluation logic. Rules are contextual guidance that fires based on conditions — path globs, tool invocations, command matches — and delivers content via one of three modes. The rules engine is a passive consumer: it does not modify prompt construction. It consumes generic path-change and tool-invocation events, tracks which rules are currently in context via a presence set rebuilt from content tags, and produces injection requests. This brief covers the types, parsing, trigger evaluation, delivery mode definitions, and lifecycle tracking mechanism. It does not implement the shell execution feature for dynamic rule content (deferred to N-008 if scoped separately).
task: Define Rule struct with RuleId, trigger conditions, delivery mode, timing (before/after), and body content. Implement rule file parsing from YAML front matter (trigger globs, command patterns, delivery mode, timing) plus plain text body. Implement TriggerCondition evaluation against RuntimeEvent (path-change and tool-invocation events). Define DeliveryMode enum with three variants matching D7. Define RulePresenceSet that tracks which rules are currently in the active context, rebuilt synchronously from content tags during prompt construction. Define RuleInjection as the output type representing a rule ready for delivery. Testing layer: unit tests (Layer 1) — all components testable with synthetic events, no provider needed.

requirements.title: Define Rule struct and RuleId
requirements.spec: The rules module SHALL define a Rule struct containing a unique RuleId, a human-readable name, a list of trigger conditions, a delivery mode, timing (before or after matched action), and the rule body content as a string. RuleId SHALL be a newtype over String with Display and FromStr implementations.
acceptance: Rule struct has fields: id (RuleId), name (String), triggers (Vec<TriggerCondition>), delivery (DeliveryMode), timing (TriggerTiming), body (String)
RuleId is a newtype over String with Display and FromStr
TriggerTiming enum has variants: Before, After
Rule derives Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize
RuleId derives Clone, Debug, Hash, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize

requirements.title: Define TriggerCondition enum
requirements.spec: TriggerCondition SHALL be an enum with variants for path glob matches and command/tool invocation matches. Path glob triggers match against file paths on read or write events. Command triggers match against bash command strings. Tool triggers match against tool invocation names. Each variant SHALL carry its match pattern as a string.
acceptance: TriggerCondition enum has variants: PathGlob { pattern: String }, BashCommand { pattern: String }, ToolInvocation { tool_name: String }
TriggerCondition derives Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize
PathGlob pattern is a glob pattern (supports *, **, ?)
BashCommand pattern is a substring match pattern for command matching
ToolInvocation tool_name is an exact tool name match

requirements.title: Define DeliveryMode enum
requirements.spec: DeliveryMode SHALL be an enum with three variants matching the three delivery modes from D7. SystemContextAppend adds the rule to the system prompt for the session. ContextInjection delivers the rule at the next input boundary. MessageDelivery sends the rule as a conversation message.
acceptance: DeliveryMode enum has variants: SystemContextAppend, ContextInjection, MessageDelivery
DeliveryMode derives Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq
Each variant carries no additional data — the rule body provides the content

requirements.title: Define RuntimeEvent for trigger evaluation
requirements.spec: The rules module SHALL define a RuntimeEvent enum representing the generic events that the rules engine consumes. The rules engine SHALL NOT subscribe to rule-specific events — it consumes generic path-change and tool-invocation events per D7's dependency inversion principle.
acceptance: RuntimeEvent enum has variants: PathChanged { path: String, operation: PathOperation }, ToolInvoked { tool_name: String, arguments: Option<serde_json::Value> }, BashCommandRun { command: String }
PathOperation enum has variants: Read, Write
RuntimeEvent derives Clone, Debug
No rule-specific event types — the rules engine is a passive consumer of generic events

requirements.title: Implement rule file parsing from YAML front matter
requirements.spec: The parser module SHALL parse rule files consisting of YAML front matter (delimited by ---) followed by a plain text body. The front matter SHALL specify trigger conditions (path globs, command patterns, tool names), delivery mode, timing, and an optional rule name. The parser SHALL return a Result<Rule, RulesError>.
acceptance: parse_rule_file function signature: fn parse_rule_file(id: RuleId, content: &str) -> Result<Rule, RulesError>
YAML front matter is delimited by --- on its own line at the start and end of the header

Front matter supports keys: name (optional String), triggers (array of trigger objects), delivery (string: system_context, context_injection, message), timing (string: before, after — defaults to before)
Trigger objects in YAML have a type field (path_glob, bash_command, tool) and a pattern field
Text after the closing --- delimiter is the rule body
Missing required fields produce RulesError::ParseFailed with a descriptive message
Cargo.toml includes serde_yaml = { workspace = true }
A unit test parses a rule file with two triggers (one path glob, one tool invocation), delivery mode context_injection, timing after, and verifies all fields

requirements.title: Implement trigger evaluation against runtime events
requirements.spec: The triggers module SHALL implement evaluation of TriggerConditions against RuntimeEvents. PathGlob triggers SHALL match using glob pattern matching against file paths. BashCommand triggers SHALL match using substring containment against command strings. ToolInvocation triggers SHALL match using exact tool name equality. Evaluation SHALL return whether the trigger matched and the timing (before/after).
acceptance: evaluate_triggers function signature: fn evaluate_triggers(rule: &Rule, event: &RuntimeEvent) -> Option<TriggerMatch>
TriggerMatch struct has fields: rule_id (RuleId), timing (TriggerTiming), delivery (DeliveryMode)
PathGlob { pattern: "**/*.rs" } matches RuntimeEvent::PathChanged { path: "src/main.rs", operation: Read }
PathGlob { pattern: "**/mod.rs" } does not match RuntimeEvent::PathChanged { path: "src/main.rs", operation: Read }
BashCommand { pattern: "cargo test" } matches RuntimeEvent::BashCommandRun { command: "cargo test --workspace" }
ToolInvocation { tool_name: "Write" } matches RuntimeEvent::ToolInvoked { tool_name: "Write", .. }
ToolInvocation { tool_name: "Write" } does not match RuntimeEvent::ToolInvoked { tool_name: "Read", .. }
Cargo.toml includes glob = { workspace = true } for glob pattern matching
A unit test exercises all three trigger types with matching and non-matching events

requirements.title: Define RulePresenceSet for lifecycle tracking
requirements.spec: The lifecycle module SHALL define a RulePresenceSet that tracks which rules are currently present in the active context. The presence set SHALL be rebuilt synchronously from content tags during each prompt construction pass. WHEN a rule was previously in context but is absent from the current tag set, AND the rule's trigger fires again, the rules engine SHALL produce a re-injection request. WHEN a rule is already in context, the engine SHALL NOT re-inject it.
acceptance: RulePresenceSet struct wraps HashSet<RuleId>
rebuild method signature: fn rebuild(&mut self, tags: &[ContentTag]) — extracts RuleIds from ContentTag::Rule variants
is_present method signature: fn is_present(&self, id: &RuleId) -> bool
Rebuilding from tags containing ContentTag::Rule("rust-conventions") marks that rule as present
Rebuilding from tags NOT containing a previously present rule marks it as absent
A unit test: build presence set with rule A present, rebuild without rule A, verify is_present returns false

requirements.title: Define RuleEngine and RuleInjection output
requirements.spec: The rules module SHALL define a RuleEngine struct that holds loaded rules and the presence set. WHEN a RuntimeEvent is submitted, the engine SHALL evaluate all rule triggers, check the presence set, and return a list of RuleInjection values for rules that matched and are not already in context. RuleInjection SHALL carry the rule ID, delivery mode, timing, and the rule body content.
acceptance: RuleEngine struct has fields: rules (Vec<Rule>), presence (RulePresenceSet)
RuleEngine::new accepts Vec<Rule>
RuleEngine::add_rule appends a rule
process_event method signature: fn process_event(&self, event: &RuntimeEvent) -> Vec<RuleInjection>
RuleInjection struct has fields: rule_id (RuleId), delivery (DeliveryMode), timing (TriggerTiming), content (String)
process_event returns empty vec when no triggers match
process_event returns empty vec when a trigger matches but the rule is already in context (presence set)
process_event returns a RuleInjection when a trigger matches and the rule is not in context
A unit test: add a rule, process a matching event twice — first returns an injection, call rebuild with the rule present, second returns empty

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement shell execution in rules for dynamic content generation — deferred to N-008
SHALL NOT implement prompt construction integration — N-003 defines ContentTag and prompt construction; this brief consumes ContentTag but does not modify prompt construction
SHALL NOT implement hook behavior (blocking, modification, interception) — rules provide information per D7
SHALL NOT implement rule file discovery or directory scanning — rules are loaded by the caller
SHALL NOT implement MCP or extension integration for rules
SHALL NOT add provider or agent loop dependencies — rules module is self-contained
verification: cargo check -p norn succeeds with zero errors
cargo test -p norn succeeds — all rules unit tests pass
cargo clippy -p norn -- -D warnings produces zero errors
grep -rn 'unwrap()\|expect(' crates/norn/src/rules/ returns no matches outside test code
Rule file parsing correctly handles YAML front matter with multiple trigger types
All three delivery modes are defined and distinguishable
PathGlob trigger correctly matches glob patterns against file paths
Presence set correctly tracks rule presence and absence across rebuilds
RuleEngine suppresses re-injection for rules already in context
